title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Control System (3 Hours)
Introduction to control systems; open-loop and closed-loop systems;
Feedback and non-feedback systems; servomechanism; history and
development of automatic control.
Feedback Characteristics of Control Systems include reduction of
parameter variation, control over system dynamics, and control of the
effects of disturbance signals.
Review of Mathematical Modelling and Transfer Function (1 Hour)
Block Diagram Algebra & Signal Flow Graphs (7 Hours)
Block diagram of an open-loop system; Block diagram of a
closed loop system; block diagram and transfer function of a
typical first-order system and second-order system.
Block diagram reduction techniques.
Concept of signal flow graph; construction of signal flow graph; Finding
transfer function from Signal flow graph using Mason’s gain formula.

title: Module 2
content:
Time-domain Analysis of First-order Systems (5 Hours)
Time response, transient and steady-state response; standard test signals
and their application in system analysis.  Open-loop and closed-loop
transfer functions, type and order of systems; the concept of poles and
zeros; type and order of the system; Response of first-order system to unit
impulse and unit step input (with derivation); Time response specifications
of first-order systems.
Time-domain Analysis of Second-order Systems (5 Hours)
Response of under-damped second-order system to unit-step input (with
derivation); Response of under-damped second-order system to unit
impulse and unit ramp input (Derivation not required); Response of over-
damped, critically damped and un-damped second-order system to unit
step input (derivation not required); time response specifications of under-
damped second order system.
Steady-state Errors and Error Constants (2 Hours)
Steady-state error, static error constants.

title: Module 3
content:
Stability of Linear Systems (4 Hours)
Stability of linear systems: Definitions, characteristic polynomial and
characteristic equation, general stability criterion with reference to s-plane;
Absolute and relative stability; necessary conditions for stability.
Routh’s criterion of stability.
The Root Locus Technique (6 Hours)
The root-locus concept; Evan’s criteria; Construction of root locus;
determination of roots from root locus; Effect of addition of zeros and
poles on root locus; root contours.

title: Module 4
content:
Frequency Response Analysis (6 Hours)
Frequency
response
representation;
sinusoidal
transfer
function;
Frequency domain specifications.
Polar plot and bode plot: Construction.
Stability in Frequency Domain (5 Hours)
Stability in frequency domain; gain margin and phase margin; stability
from bode plots.
Nyquist stability criterion; assessment of relative stability using Nyquist
criterion.
